title: In-Canada families of Canadian victims of recent air disasters – Pathway to permanent residence: How to apply - Canada.ca
content:
## In-Canada families of Canadian victims of recent air disasters – Pathway to permanent residence: How to apply
- About the public policy
This application process is for families of Canadian victims of the air disasters of flights ET302 and PS752 to apply for permanent residence. Before you start, check if you’re eligible .
Follow these steps to apply:
- Read the instruction guide
- Get your checklist and forms
- Complete your forms and gather documents
- Pay your application fees
- Submit your application
The deadline to apply was May 11, 2022
- We are no longer accepting new applications.
- If you submitted a complete application before or on May 11, 2022, we’ll continue to process it.

## Read the instruction guide
The instruction guide helps you complete the application correctly. It includes information about
- how to complete your forms
You can ask for this guide in another format .

## Get your checklist and forms
Use the document checklist to make sure you include all the forms and documents you need.
- Generic Application Form for Canada (IMM 0008) (opens in a new tab)
- Additional Dependants/Declaration (IMM 0008DEP) (opens in a new tab) , if applicable
- Schedule A: Background/Declaration (IMM 5669) (opens in a new tab)
- Additional Family Information Form – Permanent Residence (IMM 5406) (opens in a new tab)
- Document Checklist – In-Canada families of Canadian victims of recent air disasters  [IMM 0129] (opens in a new tab)
- Use of a Representative Form [IMM 5476] (opens in a new tab) , if applicable
- Authority to Release Personal Information to a Designated Individual (IMM 5475) (opens in a new tab) , if applicable
Get help to open an application form or to fill one out in the Help Centre.

## Complete your forms and gather documents
Use the instruction guide to fill out your application forms.
Follow the document checklist [IMM 0129] (opens in a new tab) to make sure you don’t forget anything.

## Pay your application fees
You have to pay your fees online . After you pay, print the receipt and include it with your application.

## Submit your application
Review the instruction guide to make sure your application is complete. If you forget something, we’ll send your application back to you without processing it.
Before you submit your application, make sure you have
- placed the checklist on top of your complete application package, as a cover to your application package
- answered all the questions on your forms
- signed your application and each form
- included your fee payment receipt
- included all the supporting documents in the order they’re listed on the checklist
To submit your application, put your application package into an envelope and label the envelope as follows:
“ Victims of air disasters public policy – VAD ”
You must apply to extend an expiring visitor record , work permit or study permit separately.

### Mail your application
Important information: Don’t use staples, binders, plastic sleeves, folders or albums to submit your application. Elastic bands or paper clips to attach photos are acceptable.
Mail your complete application to
IRCC – Humanitarian Migration Vancouver Victims of air disasters public policy – VAD 300-800 Burrard St. Vancouver, BC  V6Z 0B6
We won’t accept your application at any other office.
We’ll accept applications for this process until May 11, 2022 .

### Tracking your application package
You can use a courier service that requires a signature when delivered. This gives you a delivery confirmation and allows you to track your package.
If you need the instruction guide for this program in another format, email your request to VancouverBRO@cic.gc.ca .
We’ll only respond to emails that request an alternative guide format.
